description: Overall, it is a great tool for stats analysis. I would recommend it to any analyst.
author: Evelyn B.
pros: I like that this software is easy to use and learn. It is helpful for statistical modeling and doesnt require too much training.
cons: I do not like that it does not have as much help documentation as other tools i have used.

description: I use R Studio on a daily basis to interact with data. It's my go-to software anytime I need to do something too big for Excel or something that might need to be repeated.
author: Jason L.
pros: Such a user-friendly IDE. It makes managing all of my active data objects, visualizations, and packages extremely easy.
cons: Nobody will ever accuse R or R Studio in being the fastest or most memory-efficient software.

review_title: Do not buy this product. They treat people with disabilities like garbage.
description: I had a minor technical issue and R-Studio would not spend 30 seconds on the phone assisting me, a person with a disability who cannot process their written instructions. I don't whine about my disability. I simply ask for an accommodation, which, in my country, is required by law. I hope consumers who require data recovery software consider alternative products before giving their money to these ignorant people.
author: Rhonda S.
pros: I think it can work if you understand how to use it.
cons: The instructions are now impossible for me to understand. I have owned this software for many years; however, my disability is worse and I could not get through the first step.... Select Drive. It was always "greyed out." I phoned tech support because I have a disability... HE WOULD NOT HELP ME! I told him I cannot email because of my disability... He told me to hire a professional data recovery company. I said nothing is wrong with my drive, I am only looking for one file. He said he had another call and he hung up on me.

description: Data Visualization, Tabulation, Data Analysis
author: Aastha B.
pros: R is a great software. Its a standalone software that helps in meeting all the data analysis tasks like - Data Processing, Data Tabulation, WEighting, Data Visualization , Reporting, Data Transformation everything. To become a Data Scientist we need to understand R. Most if the features are free of cost.
cons: There are long codes if we can reduce the coding a bit and can add drag and drop features. Otherwise its a great tool.

description: I have been able to restore data for users who have no backup and have thousands of pictures and vital data.
author: Doug E.
pros: The ability to image seemingly dead hard-drives and restore data is powerful. Also the ability to connect to remote systems is another great feature.
cons: At present, it does well with the intended purpose. I have not had any issues that I would consider to be a con.

description: We have used this for several years to recover lost files and lost file systems for our customers.
author: William F.
pros: One of the most amazing features of this software is the ability to reconstruct failed RAID arrays. You can images the individual drives, then build the virtual array in the software. Once done, you can recovery files from the old array without it being intact. This alone makes this software invaluable, but it also routinely outperforms nearly any other software we compare it to.
cons: It has a slight learning curve, and it takes a little longer than most of our other recovery software options. But it works better than most, so we are ok with that.

description: Great experience, developed and deployed a lot of scripts.
author: Rajat W.
pros: Very well integrated platform to develop scripts in R. Lots of pre installed modules really helps to automate the repetitive work which are prone to human error. Since it is open source dubugging is quite easy. More user friendly than python.
cons: Not very well integrated as compared to python based platforms.

description: I have made an image classifier on both, Python and R.
author: Jay M.
pros: I work on datasets while pursuing my ambition to be a part of Data Science. The first and foremost step is to process the given data. Earlier I used to use Pycharm for all my work but R-Studio is the one tool I missed. I realized that lately. It has already inbuilt libraries which reduce the code and hence the workload by half. All you need to do is call the given library.
cons: I couldn't find any flaw as such. Even if you work on Python or R, your code must be correct. Be careful about the indexing as it is different in both. Also, sometimes python helps in detailed analysis.

description: I used R Studio for a beginning stats class. I have coding background so I figured it out pretty easily. Others in my class really struggled because even the help sites are expained with code. Be fore you use R studio, be sure to take a coding class.
author: Natalie W.
pros: R studio is pretty easy to use if you have some coding background. It was pretty easy to find help for most of my questions on the web.
cons: Some of the packages are not stable. If you are a beginner, this may not be the stats software for you.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Very useful software for data analytics and data science
author: Vahid A.
pros: I am using this software for more than 1 year and I can say it has so many useful packages which makes everything easier in data analytics and data science.
cons: I can not mention any specific problem for this software.

description: R Studio Data Recovery is a product I used during my time in University. While not necessary to operate in R, I certainly found the platform useful in learning user controls.
author: Larry B.
pros: R Studio Data Recovery makes R more user friendly and accessible.
cons: While useful, R Studio is not entirely intuitive; I anticipate many first time users would still require detailed instructions.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Setegn Bayabil A.
pros: Featurts of R software is simple and powerful for data analysis.
cons: Yes improved and basically it is simple with all

description: I've used RStudio for many years to process the data associated with my PhD research. It is particularly useful for all statistics needs.
author: Morgan D.
pros: Rstudio is extremely easy to use. My favorite feature is the plotting tab. The plotting window makes it is to refine your graphics and look at the output in real time. Additionally, the top right window "Environment" let's you check on the data you have loaded into the workspace, very convenient to have a graphic user interface to look at the data tables instead of writing in a view or print line of code.
cons: The biggest pain point for me is occasionally crashing of the program due to the large size of the files I work with.

review_title: R-Studio is a definite go to in the data recovery industry!!
description: With R-Studio being an option for our business, we are not only able to use our DDI Disk Imager for data recovery but also R-Studio. Sometimes the drive is not failed but files have just been deleted and R-Studio is the man for that job getting the deleted files back.
author: Shelby H.
pros: R-Studio is reliable, can recover deleted files, easy to use. We use R-Studio at our data recovery business "Total Access Data Recovery" so often.
cons: The only con is the cost of the program but the cost of data recovery is much higher so it paid for itself.

review_title: R-Studio is the only IDE that should be used for R
author: Rex S.
pros: I spent a lot of time trying not to use R-Studio because I like to fight against the norm. I found that there are not other competitive IDE's for R. If time is spent to learn the hot keys and enhancements in R-Studio (such as the markdown and GitHub enhancements), one will not want to use other IDEs for R.
cons: At first the product seems clunky until the shortcuts are learned. I should have watched a video for it when I was first using it.

description: work efficiently for large scale data analysis. helps in executing programmes written in R.
author: Anurag S.
pros: simplifies running the r-coded programs to run with better user interface. simpler and more efficient than normal R. have logs to check and go back in case of errors.
cons: the user interface can be better with more intuitive options making it more useful to people who are not coding savvy.

description: I enjoyed R, has been my best data science tool ever since.
author: Ismaila S.
pros: Very good programming tool for mathematician. Strong for data science and analytics task. Ever since I switched, it's no going back
cons: Coding algorithms such as neural networks can be slow in R, and users need to be familiar with mathematics to use its vector and matrices manipulation

description: I love this IDE, i am learning R these days and I am really enjoying Rstudio
author: Karan G.
pros: Ease of use, function of choosing packages, setting working directory and seeing plots while working on R
cons: Its very slow sometimes loading a code for a plot, it gets stuck and hard to kill that session, I wish it was faster

review_title: R is your savior and nemesis
description: I loved that it was free and worked well. However, it definitely made life a little bit more complicated because I just could not figure out things on my own. Do R if you have a bunch of friends jumping on it with you because it requires a lot of team effort and help.
author: Jorge M.
pros: Nothing beats free and that is for sure true with R statistics because it offers a great range of functions for free. It is such a beautiful software to do graphs and run regressions.
cons: Free, of course, comes at a price. Ironic, right? it definitely will take a long time before you feel proficient at what you are doing in "R". It requires a lot of guidance to know you are on the right track. I had to spend hours of hours with a TA to figure out how to use it at a low intermediate level.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: An excellent IDE for those who are familiar with R.
cons: When you open many script tabs, the memory bounds, or some internal problems, it crashes. In that case, using R (without IDE like R-Studio) is a better way.

description: The overall experience with R-Studio is very nice and good.
author: Prasun P.
pros: The most interesting thing about R-Studio is that it has an amazing user interface when it comes to coding in R language. With R-Studio we can get a whole lot of libraries pre installed and we can see graphs and plots very clearly with definite insight.
cons: Nothing to dislike about this software as it is the best integrated development environment for R language. More features will come in future.

author: Daniel L.
pros: R-studio is a very easy to navigate program that allows you to do every action in R but with the extra help and instruction. With this software, you can also adjust the settings to your liking so you can work comfortably. Very helpful tools allows questions to be answered quickly and informatively.
cons: R-Studio sometimes has a few too many things going on at once so it sometimes gets hard to navigate and will lose where you are working.

review_title: Very good tool for data analysis
description: Overall experience till now is good as per my uses.
author: Gulshan K.
pros: I'm just a beginner of this tool and find it very useful now. It is open source and pro version is also available. Alot of packages are available can be easily used. Git repository can easily be accessible.
cons: It is actually straight forward. Other softwares are also available in market which are much faster than R studio.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: R-Studio is great for data science. Lots of packages to use, built in charts, functions, and many more that make it friendly and easy to use, but hard to master.
author: Dennis L.
pros: It's easy to setup, powerful, hard to master. It can do MANY things regarding data science. There are also lots of packages to use which make your life easier.
cons: It is hard to pick up. The language is not universal.

description: Overall, I found it difficult to use. It has a lot of functionality and some great plotting features, but I have found other programs that are a lot easier to use that do the same tasks.
author: Marie S.
pros: I like the way you can easily put a lot of code on one line and separate work easily. The functionality of exporting makes it easier to add onto the code or even import it into already, made work, like Word documents.
cons: There's a lot of confusing aspects about this software. Sometimes, the errors make it really difficult to figure out what's wrong and there should be more help support, and online information
